name: Red Hollow Bandits
description: Former coachmen turned brigands after the western trade road collapsed. They extort the swampland villages and ambush what little traffic still uses the old highway.

name: Oak Mill Village
description: A flood-damp farming hamlet on the swamp's edge. They pay the Red Hollow in grain and silence, and wish a Crown patrol would finally ride through.

name: Kingsward Crown Patrol
description: A thin column of tabarded horse and foot sent to reassert royal writ on the western marches. Underpaid, undermanned, and two seasons late — but the tabard still carries weight where a sword alone does not.

name: Iron Vein Clan
description: A dwarven forge-clan holding an old mountain vein and the market town that grew beneath it. They trade steel and honest weights, keep the ledgers older than any kingdom, and do not forget a slight for seven generations.

name: Dune-Wind Caravan
description: A wandering polity of tent-merchants, camel-drovers, and oath-singers that crosses the eastern waste with the seasons. They owe no crown, keep their own law at the cookfire, and will ferry anything that pays — including the occasional thing a kingdom would rather stayed buried.

name: Vigil of Ashes
description: A cabal that teaches the world is already dead and only resents being told. They gather at salt-burned cairns under a long-dead star, sing down what little remains, and have begun to be correct more often than is comfortable.

name: Greenwarden Circle
description: A loose confederacy of hermits, rangers, and hedge-priests who keep the old standing-stone covenants. They are polite to travelers and unkind to axes. They will burn a cult-cairn without asking a crown for leave.
